food_subgroup: Layered sponge cake / custard cream cake

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for Hokkaido strawberry cake. FOODON_00002426 (cake food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Cakes and pastries) is the closest parent category. No specific code exists for Hokkaido-style strawberry custard cake.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Cake) is the closest available parent concept. No specific code for Hokkaido strawberry cake exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One cup serving of approximately 120 g, comprising a portion of vanilla sponge cake, fresh strawberries, strawberry sauce, and brûlée custard cream as served at Kazo Premium.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake / cream cake composite — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Cake, shortcake, biscuit-type (NDB 18101) and Cream, whipped, pressurized (NDB 01053), adapted with strawberry component from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 167762 (Strawberries, raw)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Hokkaido strawberry cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by weighted composite of: vanilla sponge cake (~55% by weight, USDA SR Legacy NDB 18101 cake shortcake), brûlée custard cream (~30% by weight, USDA SR Legacy NDB 01053 whipped cream and egg custard analogue), and fresh strawberries with sauce (~15% by weight, USDA FDC 167762). Energy and macros are cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy NDB 18139 (Cake, white, prepared from recipe without frosting) for plausibility. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Sponge cake GI = 46 (glucose reference); vanilla cake with cream and sugar GI estimated at 62 based on sugar content and cream components.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hokkaido strawberry cake. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on sponge cake GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database (sponge cake GI range 46–67, glucose scale) and the high sugar content (~22 g/100g) from strawberry sauce and brûlée custard cream. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 43.8) / 100 = 27.2, rounded to 27.1 for a 120 g serving (43.8 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~145 mg/100g; ~174 mg per cup serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of one cup serving (~174 mg) is modest and unlikely to significantly impact blood pressure when consumed occasionally, but individuals on strict sodium-restricted diets (<1500 mg/day) should account for this within their daily allowance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~22 g/100g) and medium glycemic index (~62) with high glycemic load per serving (~27)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 43.8 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~27 per cup serving, this dessert poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be consumed only occasionally in small portions with careful carbohydrate budgeting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.8 g/100g; ~8.2 g per serving) and cholesterol (~85 mg/100g; ~102 mg per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of one cup serving (~8.2 g) represents approximately 41% of the recommended daily limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk management, and individuals with established cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional small portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hokkaido Strawberry Cake (Cup) is a Japanese-inspired layered dessert sold in Singapore, popularised by specialty cake shops such as Kazo Premium. It consists of a light vanilla sponge cake base layered with fresh strawberries, a proprietary strawberry sauce, and a rich brûlée custard cream made in the Hokkaido style — a Japanese confectionery tradition known for its use of high-fat Hokkaido dairy cream. The dessert is served in individual cup portions of approximately 120 g, making it a single-serve indulgence. The texture contrasts a soft, airy sponge with the silky, slightly caramelised custard cream and the bright acidity of fresh strawberries. The flavour profile is moderately sweet with fruity and creamy notes, positioned as a less-sweet alternative to conventional cream cakes. Nutritionally, each cup provides approximately 318 kcal, 43.8 g carbohydrate (of which ~26.4 g sugar), 13.8 g fat (of which ~8.2 g saturated fat), and 5.0 g protein. The glycemic load per serving is high (~27), making this dessert a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat content is notable for cardiovascular risk management. Vitamin C from fresh strawberries (~10 mg per serving) provides a minor antioxidant contribution. This dessert is suitable for vegetarians but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for vegans or those with these allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Hokkaido strawberry cake?
Is Hokkaido strawberry cake suitable for diabetics?
What is in a Hokkaido strawberry cake?
How much fat in a cup of Hokkaido cake?
Hokkaido cake vs regular cream cake nutrition

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Hokkaido strawberry cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for vanilla sponge cake, whipped custard cream, and fresh strawberries. GI is estimated from analogous sponge cake values in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis or approved-source exact match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. All other -1 values have been replaced with estimated numeric values where defensible. Serving size of 120 g is estimated based on typical single-cup dessert portion sizes for this product category; no official manufacturer weight declaration was available. Halal status is listed as false because no halal certification information is available for Kazo Premium - Funan; consumers requiring halal-certified products should verify directly with the restaurant. The food_name has been normalised from the GrabFood menu string '1 x (Cup) Signature Hokkaido Strawberry Cake' to the canonical form 'Hokkaido Strawberry Cake (Cup)' per Veritas naming rules. Iteration 3 fix: aliases consistency audit performed — all 23 string values present in aliases_by_language (across english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi, japanese, and romanized keys) have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. The romanized alias 'Bak Hoi Do Cao Mei Dan Gao (Bei Zhuang)' was confirmed present in both aliases_by_language.romanized and the flat aliases array.

Iteration 1: Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual local_names (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Iteration 1: Built composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy sources with full provenance documentation.
Iteration 1: Added GI estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake analogues.
Iteration 1: Added three ontology anchors with correct match_type values (closest_parent only; no invented codes).
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, selenium_mcg) in notes field.
Iteration 2: Added mandatory Japanese local_name ('北海道ストロベリーケーキ') and corresponding Japanese aliases due to Hokkaido inspiration.
Iteration 2: Corrected Q1 and Q8 issues from iteration 1.

Iteration 3: Performed exhaustive aliases consistency audit — enumerated every string value across all aliases_by_language language keys and verified each appears verbatim in the flat aliases array. Confirmed 'Bak Hoi Do Cao Mei Dan Gao (Bei Zhuang)' present in both aliases_by_language.romanized and flat aliases. All 23 alias strings confirmed consistent. This resolves the q8 failure reported by the validator for iteration 2.
